brief_document.title: Timer NIFs (sleep, start_timer, cancel_timer, with_timeout)
brief_document.purpose: Implement the timer NIFs that workflow code calls for durable sleep, named timer management, and timeout wrapping. Each NIF goes through the durability handoff: on replay, recorded timer events are returned without scheduling real timers; on live execution, the NIF delegates to AT's timer scheduling services (TimerService, SleepTimer, named timer API) through the engine's DelegatedSeams (D4) and records the durable events through the Recorder.
brief_document.task: Create nif_timer.rs with production NIF implementations for sleep/1, start_timer/2, cancel_timer/1, and with_timeout/2. Each NIF constructs a NifContext (NB-001), builds the appropriate Command, calls resolve_or_execute_live. On replay, return the recorded result. On live, delegate to AT's concrete timer services (D4), record events through the Recorder (CO2), and return the result. Replace the stubs in engine_nifs.rs.

requirements.title: sleep/1 NIF records TimerScheduled and blocks until TimerFired
requirements.spec: WHEN sleep/1 is called on the live path, THE SYSTEM SHALL record TimerScheduled through the Recorder, delegate to AT's sleep service to schedule the timer, block the dirty NIF thread until the timer fires or is cancelled, and record TimerFired through the Recorder (CO2). WHEN called during replay, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the recorded TimerFired result without scheduling a real timer (C18).
acceptance: nif_timer.rs implements sleep_impl that constructs a NifContext, resolves the sleep Command, and on ResumeLive delegates to AT's sleep function (crate::time::sleep or equivalent).
On the live path, TimerScheduled is recorded before the sleep, and TimerFired is recorded when the timer fires.
On the replay path, the recorded TimerFired is returned immediately without blocking.
A test records a sleep's full event trail on the live path and asserts TimerScheduled + TimerFired events appear in the store; a replay test asserts the NIF returns immediately.

requirements.title: start_timer/2 NIF records TimerScheduled and returns timer ID
requirements.spec: WHEN start_timer/2 is called on the live path, THE SYSTEM SHALL record TimerScheduled through the Recorder, delegate to AT's start_timer service, and return the TimerId to the BEAM process. WHEN called during replay, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the recorded TimerId without scheduling a real timer (C18).
acceptance: nif_timer.rs implements start_timer_impl that takes a timer name and duration, constructs a Command, resolves it, and on live delegates to crate::time::start_timer.
TimerScheduled is recorded with the timer name and duration.
The TimerId is returned as a binary term to the BEAM process.
A test asserts TimerScheduled is recorded on the live path and the TimerId is returned; a replay test asserts the recorded TimerId is returned without scheduling.

requirements.title: cancel_timer/1 NIF records TimerCancelled
requirements.spec: WHEN cancel_timer/1 is called on the live path, THE SYSTEM SHALL record TimerCancelled through the Recorder and delegate to AT's cancel_timer service to disarm the timer. WHEN called during replay, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the recorded result without disarming a real timer (C18).
acceptance: nif_timer.rs implements cancel_timer_impl that takes a TimerId, constructs a Command, resolves it, and on live delegates to crate::time::cancel_timer.
TimerCancelled is recorded with the TimerId.
The NIF returns {ok, cancelled} to the BEAM process.
A test asserts TimerCancelled is recorded on the live path; a replay test asserts the recorded result is returned without service interaction.

requirements.title: with_timeout/2 NIF wraps an operation with a deadline
requirements.spec: WHEN with_timeout/2 is called on the live path, THE SYSTEM SHALL schedule a deadline timer and execute the wrapped operation. IF the operation completes first, THE SYSTEM SHALL cancel the deadline timer and return the result. IF the deadline fires first, THE SYSTEM SHALL record the timeout event and return {error, timeout} to the BEAM process. WHEN called during replay, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the recorded outcome (C18).
acceptance: nif_timer.rs implements with_timeout_impl that takes a duration and an operation descriptor, schedules a deadline timer through AT's service, and races the operation against the deadline.
If the operation wins, the deadline timer is cancelled and the result is returned.
If the deadline wins, a timeout event is recorded and {error, timeout} is returned.
On replay, the recorded outcome (either the operation result or the timeout) is returned without scheduling or executing.
A test verifies the operation-wins path records the correct events; a second test verifies the timeout-wins path.

requirements.title: engine_nifs.rs wiring for timer NIFs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL replace the sleep/1, start_timer/2, cancel_timer/1, and with_timeout/2 stub entries in engine_nifs.rs with calls to nif_timer.rs implementations.
acceptance: The NIF functions registered in engine_nif_entries() for the four timer NIFs delegate to nif_timer implementations.
All four timer NIFs are registered as dirty (they block on timer service calls via block_on).
The stub {error, not_yet_implemented} responses are removed for these four NIFs.

boundaries: SHALL NOT reimplement AT's timer scheduling, wheel management, or durable timer recovery — those are consumed via the existing time module API (D4).
SHALL NOT bypass the Recorder to call EventStore::append directly (CO2).
SHALL NOT bypass the Resolver to skip replay checks (CO1).
SHALL NOT import beamr outside the runtime module (CO8).
SHALL NOT add wall-clock reads in timer NIFs — durations come from the workflow; timestamps come from recorded events.
verification: Live path records TimerScheduled/TimerFired/TimerCancelled events in the correct order.
Replay path returns recorded results without scheduling real timers.

checklist.text: sleep/1 NIF records TimerScheduled, blocks until the timer fires or is cancelled, and records TimerFired.
start_timer/2 NIF records TimerScheduled and returns the timer ID.
cancel_timer/1 NIF records TimerCancelled for the named timer.
with_timeout/2 NIF wraps an operation with a deadline; records timeout expiry if the deadline fires first.
Timer NIFs on replay return recorded results without scheduling real timers.

stories.text: As a workflow author, I want to sleep for a duration and have the engine wake my workflow after that time so that I can implement polling, backoff, and scheduled delays.

constraints.text: Every NIF goes through resolve_or_execute_live (or its equivalent replay check). No NIF may execute a side effect without first checking recorded history.
All event recording goes through the workflow's single-writer Recorder. No NIF calls EventStore::append directly.
Deterministic execution: workflow.now returns recorded timestamp, workflow.random is seeded from WorkflowId+RunId. No wall clock or entropy source in NIF-visible paths.
The aion crate remains transport-agnostic. Remote worker dispatch stays in aion-server's WorkerActivityDispatcher; the NIF bridge calls the ActivityDispatcher trait, not the concrete server type.
unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in any new code.
Only the runtime module imports beamr. NIF bridge code uses RuntimeHandle, not beamr directly.
Tests run against InMemoryStore. No test requires a database or live server.

resolved_context.intention: Connect the last mile: the BEAM process calls aion_flow_ffi NIFs, and those NIFs go through the durability handoff so every side effect is recorded, replayed on recovery, and deterministic. When this cluster lands, a workflow that starts, runs activities on remote workers, sleeps, receives signals, spawns children, and completes produces a full, replayable event history — the Temporal developer expectation, not just for basic cases, but for all of them.
